rulings.Case Title: KAITLYN JOHNSON ET AL VS. KATHLEEN MCHUGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment, Order Civil Contempt And/Or Appoint A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER KAITLYN JOHNSON, CAROLINE GASPARINI Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment, Order Civil Contempt And/Or Appoint A Receiver is GRANTED, in part. The deodar cedar tree in the Common Area of the HOA, which was the subject of the Arbitration Award and Judgment entered in this matter on October 23, 2024 shall be removed no later than May 9, 2025. Costs for the removal of the subject tree shall be borne by the nominal Respondent, the 217 / 219 Divisadero Street Homeowners Association and/or split equally between Petitioners and Respondents. A compliance hearing shall be set in this matter for May 16, 2025. If the subject deodar cedar tree in the Common Area of the HOA is not removed by May 9, 2025, then at the May 16, 2026 compliance hearing the Court shall hold a hearing to determine whether Patrick J. Connolly, Esq. shall be appointed a Receiver to carry out the terms of the Judgment, to wit: remove the subject tree. Respondent's objection to Exhibits 6 and 8 of the Lightfoot Declaration is overruled. Petitioner's request to set a hearing for civil contempt is denied without prejudice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys' Fees And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon Against Defendant Rothenberg Ventures Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, CRAIG HORDLOW Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys' Fees And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon Against Defendant Rothenberg Ventures Llc is DENIED. Although Plaintiff styles its motion as a Motion for Entry of Judgement, Plaintiff failed to cite any authority, statutory or otherwise, in its moving papers or reply authorizing the court to enter judgment against Defendant Rothenberg Ventures LLC on the breach of contract claim. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees for the breach of contract claim is denied. Plaintiff has not established that it is the prevailing party as to the breach of contract claim as there are no rulings, orders, or judgement on file. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees as to the fraud claim is denied. Plaintiff failed to establish entitlement to fees under CCP section 1717, which entitles the prevailing party to fees in any action on a contract that includes an attorney fee provision. Plaintiff's argument that the fraud claim was necessary or that the Note was part of a fraudulent transaction is not persuasive. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Tolling And Or Extending Time To Bring Action To Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declarations Of Dana Delman And Craig Hordlow In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, CRAIG HORDLOW Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Tolling And Or Extending Time To Bring Action To Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declarations Of Dana Delman And Craig Hordlow In Support Thereof is GRANTED. The 5-year deadline is extended as to Michael Rothenberg to account for tolling between June 25, 2020 through February 1, 2024 (1,683 days), which would bring the new 5-year deadline to November 17, 2029. The Court finds that it was impractical to bring the case against Michael Rothenberg to trial during this time due to the criminal case pending against him. The 5-year deadline is extended as to Rothenberg Ventures LLC to account for tolling between February 14, 2025 through April 9, 2025 (55 days), which would bring the new 5-year deadline to June 3, 2025. The Court finds that it would have been futile to bring the claims against RVMC to trial after the fraud claim was dismissed for the sole reason that there is nothing left to bring to trial as to RVMC. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSA ISELA FRANCO VS. CASEY MCLOED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: 2renewed Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO 2renewed Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar is DENIED. The court does not find that the attorney neglect was excusable for the reason that even if counsel had instructed her assistant to attach a copy of the settlement agreement to the dismissal, that still would not have been enough for the court to retain jurisdiction. The court further notes that the application for relief was made more than 6 months ago. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY FSB, VS. DAGOBERTO FLORES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2025 line 6. PLAINTIFF WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY FSB, NOT IN ITS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY, BUT SOLELY AS OWNER TRUSTEE FOR BRAVO RESIDENTIAL FUNDING TRUST 2021-NQM1 MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to the amount of rent due. Summary adjudication is denied for failure to comply with CRC 1.1350(b) because the issues in the Notice are not repeated verbatim in the Separate Statement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
